hypothesis: If pre-trained single-gas neural networks are used as feature extractors within a graph neural network architecture that explicitly encodes gas identity as nodes and mixture composition as edge/node attributes, then the resulting model will more accurately and generalizably predict mixture plasma etching rates across unseen process conditions and gas compositions compared to a standard feedforward neural network trained directly on mixture data, because the graph structure leverages transferable single-gas physical knowledge while the message-passing mechanism captures synergistic inter-gas interactions in the mixture.
